content:
- Completed the task end-to-end: shared postmaster ADT extracted, API reload endpoint now sequences API cert reload then managed postmaster SIGHUP, tests/docs/task metadata updated.
- Validation is green on the final tree: make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long all passed, and I reran lint after the docs reference update to keep the final tree green.
- Next step is only repository closeout: git add/commit with the required task-finished message and git push.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md after loading the Choose-Task progress log.
- Confirmed the active backlog currently contains a single unblocked failing task: .ralph/tasks/story-logging-simplification/01-task-rewrite-logging-around-private-typed-events-and-json-postgres-defaults.md.
- Next step, if resumed, would be unnecessary for this agent because the selection is now being written to .ralph/current_task.txt and this agent should quit immediately.

- Read progress log and current task pointer; active task is .ralph/tasks/story-logging-simplification/01-task-rewrite-logging-around-private-typed-events-and-json-postgres-defaults.md.
- Opened the task file and found it still marked <status>not_started</status> <passes>false</passes> even though prior progress says implementation and all required validation already passed.
- Observed worktree changes only in Ralph bookkeeping files so far; next step is to verify whether the task doc has a NOW EXECUTE marker / closeout state and inspect recent commits before deciding whether this turn is just metadata + commit/push.

- Verified the active branch still has the old logging surface: src/logging/mod.rs re-exports AppEvent/AppEventHeader/StructuredFields and raw record builders, and emitter modules still call fields_mut()/append_json_map.
- This means the task file is effectively still in the design phase despite the stale prior log entry. I am now doing the required all-types-first pass before any compile-fix work.
- Spawned focused explorers to inventory emitter event shapes and postgres_ingest responsibilities while I inspect the logging modules locally.

- Inspected src/logging/postgres_ingest.rs, src/logging/raw_record.rs, src/logging/event.rs, and src/logging/mod.rs without editing anything.
- Confirmed postgres_ingest currently mixes three concerns: polling/tailing + cleanup orchestration, line decoding/parsing into raw records, and internal app-event emission for worker health/retry telemetry.
- Identified current builder dependencies as AppEvent/AppEventHeader construction on the internal-event side and PostgresLineRecordBuilder -> RawRecordBuilder on the raw-line side; will summarize a plausible typed-event split around worker, source discovery/cleanup, line decoding/parsing, and event encoding modules.

- Inspected current emitter call sites in src/api/worker.rs, src/dcs/worker.rs, src/pginfo/worker.rs, src/process/worker.rs, and src/runtime/node.rs.
- Confirmed api/worker currently does not call emit_app_event or emit_raw_record; it only stores LogHandle in ApiServerCtx/ApiAppState and uses tower_http TraceLayer.
- Extracted logging shapes: dcs uses app events with base scope/member_id plus per-event fields error/applied/store_healthy/trust_prev/trust_next/command; pginfo uses app events with member_id plus error or sql transition fields; process uses many app events plus raw subprocess line records keyed by job_id/job_kind/binary and optional fields data_dir/error/stream/bytes_len; runtime/node emits a single startup app event with scope/member_id/startup_run_id/logging.level.
- Next step is to produce a concise but detailed typed-ADT-oriented summary with file references and distinct event variants/field unions.

- Completed the required design-only pass for the logging refactor. Replaced src/logging/event.rs with typed ADTs: top-level LogEvent, domain event enums (Runtime/Dcs/PgInfo/Process/PostgresIngest), typed subprocess/postgres line events, and typed identity structs.
- Narrowed the logging handle surface in src/logging/mod.rs to a single emit(origin, LogEvent) entry point and removed the public mutable app/raw emit methods from the boundary. Replaced raw_record.rs builder types with an internal EncodedRecord placeholder so the old record-construction API is gone from the public logging surface.
- Started propagating the new types into emitter modules: runtime now constructs a typed startup event, dcs/pginfo helper signatures now expect typed events, process helpers now pivot toward ProcessJobIdentity/ProcessExecutionIdentity and subprocess lines are modeled as SubprocessLineEvent, postgres_ingest now pivots to PostgresIngestEvent/PostgresLineEvent/PostgresLineSource.
- The tree is intentionally not compiling yet. I did not run make check/lint/test/test-long because the user instructions for the no-marker/TO-BE-VERIFIED phase require changing all types first, then replacing the task tail with NOW EXECUTE, then quitting immediately.
- Appended NOW EXECUTE to the active task file. Next turn should fix the resulting compiler/type errors, finish the refactor execution, then run make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, and only after that handle docs/task closeout.

- Read the current task pointer and active task file. The task already ends with NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is the compile/fix/validate phase, not another design-only pass.
- Confirmed the worktree contains in-progress logging refactor edits in src/logging/* plus dcs/pginfo/process/runtime call sites. Next I am reading those files and compiling the state of the refactor before patching.
- Plan for this turn: inspect current typed-event changes, finish the migration, run make check/lint/test/test-long, then do docs and Ralph closeout only after validation is green.

- Replaced src/logging/event.rs wholesale with a real typed-event encoder. It now maps runtime/dcs/pginfo/process/postgres-ingest/postgres-line/subprocess-line events into final LogRecord attributes instead of returning the temporary “not wired yet” error.
- Decided to preserve the prior record shape where sensible: internal events still encode event.name/event.domain/event.result plus the old field keys, but only the logging module now knows that mapping.
- A worker finished the remaining DCS/PGInfo conversion in its branch; I still need to apply or recreate those edits locally if they are not already present in this worktree. Next I am patching process/worker and logging tests against the new typed API.

- make check and make lint are now green after wiring the typed encoder, migrating runtime/dcs/pginfo/process/postgres_ingest emitters, renaming the test-only direct emit helper, and rewriting logging/postgres-ingest tests off the deleted AppEvent/raw-record APIs.
- The remaining big gates are make test and make test-long. If those fail, likely hotspots are the new typed attribute expectations, postgres ingest integration behavior, or managed-postgres config defaults/docs that still need aligning.
- After tests are green I still need to verify/update managed PostgreSQL JSON logging defaults and then do docs via the k2-docs-loop skill before Ralph closeout.

- Final tree is green after the logging refactor and the managed-postgres JSON logging default change. I reran make check, make lint, make test, and make test-long after the last code changes; all passed.
- Updated docs/reference/runtime-configuration.md and docs/explanation/process-management.md to reflect the managed JSON logging defaults, reserved extra_guc keys, and the typed subprocess logging boundary. I used the k2-docs-loop skill entrypoint, but its generic choose-doc result targeted an unrelated TLS tutorial, so I applied the actual feature docs updates directly as the factual fallback.
- Marked the Ralph task done with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria. Next steps are only task switch, git add/commit with the required task-finished message, and git push.
